spec_match.rationale: The page demonstrates reading dropEffect during dragenter and dragleave to update a drop zone before a drop occurs.

interactive.rationale: The page now includes clickable controls that invoke the same runtime hook as dragenter and expose copy, move, link, none, and dragleave states.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept remains aligned with the feature-index promise of styling the drop zone based on the effective drop operation.

edge_cases.rationale: The simulated source control includes a blocked drag state, and the dragleave control verifies that Chrome 148 resets dropEffect to none.

references.rationale: The references section includes chromestatus.com/feature/6325617459068928 and the HTML drag-and-drop events spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: The edited controls use shared CSS variables and pair button background overrides with explicit colors.

summary: The effective drop detection page now has live controls, edge-case coverage, and a labelled references section, with no remaining major findings.
